economic_notes: GBEs include Umbata Falls LP (hydro), Twin Falls LP (hydro), Biiwaabikoke LP (mining labour services), MKWA Contracting LP (road maintenance/forestry), BMI-Maamwigaaboo Contracting LP (forestry/mining/construction), Wiigiiwaaganag LP (commercial real estate). Consulting revenue of $1.5M earned from Bamkushwada LP and East-West Tie LP related to share purchase transaction. MKWA Contracting LP changed from governmental unit to GBE status during the year.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to non-compliance with PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations — management has not completed assessment of tangible capital assets for potential AROs, impact cannot be quantified. Three consolidated subsidiaries: Biigtigong Dbenjgan Ltd., Begetekong Trust, KI Ltd. Six GBEs/GBPs accounted for by modified equity method. MKWA Contracting LP changed from consolidated governmental unit to GBE during the year, resulting in deconsolidation — prospective adjustment reduced TCA by $1,503,875 and reduced accumulated surplus by $1,531,567. Large deferred revenue balance of $41.9M primarily from ISC water and sewer capital funding ($27.75M) and education centre ($1.63M). RBC term loan of $7.79M secured by future land claim settlement proceeds, nominally due July 2024. Significant TCA increase driven by $39.4M acquisitions including large construction-in-progress. Total government transfers calculated as federal ($39,261,677) + provincial ($7,146,882) = $46,408,559. OSR includes Ontario First Nation LP, KEB, capacity funding, rent, UOI, consulting fees, interest, and other revenue lines.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
